text: This PR addresses 40 of 46 performance audit findings with mostly correct implementations. Dynamic imports, useMemo wraps, scaleX animations, and import cleanups are well-executed. However, 5 regressions were introduced: a critical security bypass in ProtectedRoute via substring role matching, 2 invisible progress bars in TierPathVisualizer and 4 landing components due to missing CSS, and 2 unfixed issues (DashboardShell React keys, recharts barrel imports). 2 important issues (inline callbacks defeating memo, staleTime ineffective for polling) and 2 minor issues (UserTable date formatting, BlogPostForm onChange reference) persist.

reasoning: 3 critical regressions: (1) ProtectedRoute uses String.includes() for role auth — a substring bypass allows ADMIN to access ORG_ADMIN routes; (2) TierPathVisualizer fill bar is invisible because absolutely positioned element has no base width; (3) 4 landing components (CTA, ImpactStats, LevelPathway, MissionVision) lost their slide-up animation because transition-transform was dropped. 2 important issues: DashboardShell stat/action keys still use raw labels, recharts barrel imports unfixed. All are straightforward to fix.

message: ThemeToggleButton correctly extracted from inline definition to a module-level import from @/components/theme/ThemeToggle, eliminating the unmount/remount cycle on every render.
Namespace import * as Sentry correctly replaced with named import { captureException }, and relative import updated to @/lib/api path alias.
O(n²) levels.find() inside levels.map() correctly refactored — currentTier computed once before the map, replacing O(n²) lookups with a single O(n) pass.
transition-width correctly replaced with transform: scaleX() + origin-left + overflow-hidden, avoiding layout-triggering width animation.
Width animation correctly converted to GPU-composited transform: scaleX() with overflow-hidden wrapper.
Same correct scaleX() conversion for the progress bar animation.
ToolbarButton wrapped in React.memo, onUpdate in useCallback, and all toolbar handlers in stable useCallback refs — correctly addresses the re-render audit findings.
RichTextEditor correctly dynamically imported via next/dynamic(() => import('./RichTextEditor'), { ssr: false }), lazy-loading ~80-100KB of tiptap only when the form is opened.
Blanket watch() replaced with per-field watch() calls and individual useMemo dependencies, fixing both the stale object reference and full-form re-render.
EventSeriesForm correctly dynamically imported via next/dynamic with ssr:false, preventing ~440 lines of form logic from being bundled unnecessarily.
chartData correctly wrapped in useMemo with [data] dependency, preventing array recreation on every render.
VolunteerRow correctly extracted as a memo() component, providing the structural foundation for render optimization.
loading="lazy" correctly added to next/Image for below-fold badge images.
Date formatting correctly moved from inline new Date().toLocaleDateString() to useMemo with [orgs] dependency.
selectedUser lookup correctly moved from inline IIFE to useMemo with [users, openMenu] deps.
Date formatting correctly memoized with useMemo, eliminating toLocaleDateString calls on every render.
Mobile menu correctly changed from conditional mount {mobileOpen && ...} to Tailwind hidden/block classes, keeping DOM mounted.
sizes prop correctly matches the md:grid-cols-3 responsive layout: 100vw mobile, 50vw tablet, 33vw desktop.
blur-3xl reduced to blur-xl with will-change-transform to reduce GPU compositing cost.
Duplicate guard (!certifications.includes(trimmed)) prevents React key collisions from duplicate certification entries.
Separate type and value imports from @/lib/shared correctly combined into single import with inline type keyword.
toggle function correctly wrapped in useCallback with [setTheme, resolvedTheme] deps.
disableTransitionOnChange correctly removed, eliminating jarring instant flash on theme swap.

rolesKey.includes(user.role) uses String.includes() for substring matching instead of Array.includes() for exact comparison. A user with role 'ADMIN' can access routes restricted to 'ORGANIZATION_ADMIN' because 'ORGANIZATION_ADMIN'.includes('ADMIN') === true. This is a security regression introduced by the fix.
suggestion: Replace with allowedRoles.includes(user.role) and add allowedRoles to useEffect dependency array. The original issue (array reference on every render) should be fixed by stabilizing the parent's allowedRoles prop with useMemo, not by converting to a string.

message: The connection fill bar is absolutely positioned with left:0 but no right:0 or w-full. For absolutely positioned non-replaced elements, width defaults to shrink-to-fit — zero for an empty div. transform: scaleX(0.5) applied to a zero-width element produces zero, so the progress bar is invisible regardless of completion percentage.
suggestion: Add right-0 to the className so the absolutely positioned div stretches to fill the container width, giving scaleX() a measurable base width to operate on.

message: transition-all replaced with transition-opacity only, but the component also animates translate-y (slide-up). The translate-y changes now snap instantly instead of animating, breaking the visual effect. Same issue in ImpactStats:19, LevelPathway:47, MissionVision:31.
suggestion: Add motion-safe:transition-transform to preserve the slide-up animation: motion-safe:transition-opacity motion-safe:transition-transform motion-safe:duration-700

message: stat.label still used as raw React key without unique suffix — if two stat cards have identical labels (e.g., two 'Active' entries), keys would collide causing rendering bugs. Also applies to QuickAction keys at lines 178 and 196.
suggestion: Use key={`${stat.label}-${index}`} or require a unique id field.

message: Still imports recharts components from the top-level barrel (from 'recharts') instead of tree-shakeable sub-path imports per the audit. HoursBarChart.tsx:7 has the same unaddressed issue.
suggestion: Verify if Next.js can tree-shake recharts barrel imports. If not, switch to sub-path imports (e.g., 'recharts/es6/pie/Pie') or document the bundler behavior. Dynamic import at the consumer level is already in place.

message: VolunteerRow is memo() but all 4 callbacks (onToggle, onHoursChange, onRatingChange, onApprove) are inline arrow functions created on every render of AttendanceChecklist. React.memo's shallow comparison detects new prop references, so every row re-renders on any state change — defeating the purpose of memoization.
suggestion: Wrap each callback in useCallback with proper dependencies, or accept that memoization here provides limited benefit without wider callback stabilization.

message: staleTime:30000 matches refetchInterval:30000. With TanStack Query, refetchInterval polls regardless of staleTime, so no polling is prevented. The billing/performance issue remains.
suggestion: Use a higher staleTime (e.g. 60000) or implement document.visibilitychange to pause polling when the tab is backgrounded.

message: content is only used as the initial value in useEditor's options. If the parent changes content after mount (e.g. form reset, editing existing post), the editor won't reflect the change — TipTap requires calling editor.commands.setContent() in a useEffect to sync external changes.
suggestion: Add a useEffect with [content, editor] deps that calls editor.commands.setContent(content) when content changes after the initial render, guarded against infinite loops.

message: new Date(u.createdAt).toLocaleDateString(...) runs on every render for every row — same pattern as OrganizationTable.tsx's fix but not addressed here.
suggestion: Pre-format dates in a memoized array, same pattern as the OrganizationTable fix in this PR.

message: Inline onChange function passed to RichTextEditor creates a new reference every render. This means RichTextEditor's handleUpdate useCallback receives a new [onChange] dependency each time, partially defeating the optimization. Pre-existing pattern, not a regression.
suggestion: Wrap the onChange callback in useCallback to provide a stable reference.

message: useEffect dependency is [volunteers.length] instead of [volunteers]. If volunteers array content changes with same length (e.g. updated check-in times), the effect won't re-run and state won't update.
suggestion: Use [volunteers] as the dependency, or serialize the relevant fields. The current approach trades correctness for a minor performance gain.
